document.title: SAFETY TEST REPORT IEC 62368-1:2014 (Second Edition) and/or EN 62368-1:2014

sourceSummary.oneLine: Sporton International Inc. safety test report L060512L230 for Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C-P and ST60-2230C-PU modules against IEC 62368-1:2014 and EN 62368-1:2014+A11:2017.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports safety conformity evidence for audio/video, information and communication technology equipment, with final installation evaluation required because the equipment is building-in type.
keyPoints: Report issued by Sporton International Inc. on 2020-09-17.
Applicant is Laird Connectivity, Inc.; manufacturer is same as applicant.
Test object is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 plus Bluetooth 4.2 module with PCIe/UART interface.
Models covered are ST60-2230C-P and ST60-2230C-PU.
The equipment is Class III and all circuits are ES1.
Overall compliance, accessibility, enclosures, and several safeguards must be evaluated in the final end-use installation.

evidence.claim: The report number is L060512L230.
The report was issued on 2020-09-17.
The testing laboratory was Sporton International Inc. Safety Lab.
evidence.evidenceText: Testing laboratory .............................. : Sporton International Inc. (Safety Lab.)

evidence.claim: The applicant was Laird Connectivity, Inc.
The manufacturer was the same as the applicant.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer ..................................... : Same as applicant

evidence.claim: The test object was an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 plus Bluetooth 4.2 module with PCIe/UART interface.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of test object ............................. : 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module PCIe/UART interface

evidence.claim: The report covers model ST60-2230C-P.
The report covers model ST60-2230C-PU.
The rated supply is DC 3.3 V.
The applicable standards listed are IEC 62368-1:2014 and EN 62368-1:2014+A11:2017.
The equipment is intended for use with information technology equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is a 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module PCIe/UART interface, intended for used with information technology equipment.

evidence.claim: The equipment is building-in type and final installation compliance must be evaluated.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is classified as building-in type, the overall compliance shall be investigated and evaluated in final installation.

evidence.claim: Accessibility for ordinary persons must be evaluated in the end-use product.
evidence.evidenceText: The accessibility for ordinary person shall be evaluated in the end-use product.

evidence.claim: Electrical, fire, mechanical, and thermal safeguards are to be evaluated and provided in the end-use equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment needs for suitable Electrical enclosure (for ES safeguard), fire enclosure (for PS safeguard), mechanical enclosure (for MS safeguard), and safeguard for thermal burn injury (for TS safeguard) is to be evaluated and provided in the end-use equipment.

evidence.claim: The test sample was pre-production without serial number.
evidence.evidenceText: The test sample is pre-production without serial number.

evidence.claim: DC 3.3 V input and all circuits are classified ES1.
evidence.evidenceText: DC3.3 V-in and all circuits ES1

evidence.claim: The equipment is Class III equipment and all circuits are ES1.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is classified as Class III equipment, all circuits are ES1 circuit.

evidence.claim: All conductors and devices are considered potential ignition sources.
evidence.evidenceText: All conductors and devices are considered as PIS.

evidence.claim: The PWB critical component requirement is V-1 or better and minimum 105 ºC under UL 796.
evidence.evidenceText: PWB Interchangeable Interchangeable V-1 or better, min. 105 ºC. UL 796 UL

evidence.claim: For ST60-2230C-P, PWB near U3 measured 89.6 ºC with allowed Tmax 105 ºC.
evidence.evidenceText: Test module: ST60-2230C-P ... PWB near U3 89.6 -- -- -- 105

evidence.claim: For ST60-2230C-PU, PWB near U3 measured 86.6 ºC with allowed Tmax 105 ºC.
evidence.evidenceText: Test module: ST60-2230C-PU ... PWB near U3 86.6 -- -- -- 105

evidence.claim: The European group differences attachment is according to EN 62368-1:2014+A11:2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Differences according to ……………: EN 62368-1:2014+A11:2017

evidence.claim: The attachment originator is Nemko AS.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Originator ………………: Nemko AS

evidence.claim: EU electrical and electronic equipment substance restrictions are referenced through Directive 2011/65/EU.
evidence.evidenceText: NOTE Z1 The use of certain substances in electrical and electronic equipment is restricted within the EU: see Directive 2011/65/EU.

evidence.claim: Non-ionizing radiation from radio frequencies from 0 to 300 GHz is regulated by European Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: The amount of non-ionizing radiation is regulated by European Council Recommendation 1999/519/EC of 12 July 1999 on the limitation of exposure of the general public to electromagnetic fields (0 Hz to 300 GHz).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text supports IEC/EN, CENELEC, and EU regulatory references rather than FDA authority.
No explicit RA submission status, certificate number, or regulatory approval status is present; the document is a safety test report.
Several end-use safety evaluations are deferred because the equipment is building-in type.
